tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, low total CO₂, high chloride, and significant proteinuria, suggesting possible renal dysfunction with metabolic acidosis. Urine is abnormal in appearance and color, with high protein and bilirubin, and low specific gravity. HbA1c is low, but glucose is normal. Lipid profile shows mildly elevated cholesterol and LDL. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for underlying renal and hepatic pathology.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
